Groww is one of the most beginner-friendly Android investment apps I’ve used. The interface is clean, SIP setup for mutual funds is simple, and buying stocks/ETFs is straightforward. Portfolio tracking across investments is convenient, and onboarding is mostly digital. The trade-off is that research tools and advanced trading features are not as deep as specialized trading platforms. For long-term investors and first-time users, it’s an excellent choice.

I am extremely disappointed with the Grow app. It is a stock broker, but when I added 100 rupees, I had a 20 rupee loss, which, due to the broker’s fault, escalated to an 800 rupee loss. They took my limit order and treated it as a market order, and the stop loss hit too quickly, even though the price hadn’t reached that point. I advise everyone not to trade on this app, because no matter how many limit orders you place, they get executed as market orders, and the stop loss hits instantly witho

Hello Groww app team, As you are aware, recent regulatory changes have made the TER of a mutual fund a highly volatile quantity. You need to urgently display the base expense ratio (BER) for better context. The TER id the BER plus brokerage costs plus taxes, and a single day of heavy trading by the fund manager caused the TER to misrepresent the actual value. This also masks actual increases in costs to the customer.
